subject: Geology
title: Stratigraphy
description: A quick, practical guide to Stratigraphy.

hook.text: Stack of pancakes: the bottom one was made first. Rock layers work the same way. Oldest is on the bottom.

buildup.text: Stratigraphy is the study of rock layers, called strata, to understand the sequence of geologic events.

discovery.text: Steno's law of superposition says undisturbed layers get younger upward. It's geology's simplest rule.

twist.text: But layers get folded, faulted, and eroded. Reading them requires detective work, not just stacking logic.

climax.text: Stratigraphy turns cliffs and canyon walls into history books. Each layer is a chapter of Earth's story.

punchline.text: Read the layers, read the past.

quiz.question: What does the law of superposition state?
options: Older layers are always on top
In undisturbed layers, the oldest is at the bottom
All rock layers are the same age
